title: Graph Search
description: The code graph contains symbols and specs. The specd graph search command provides relevance-ranked search across both through abstract code-graph search capabilities.

rules: The command accepts a search query and optional flags for category filtering, kind filtering, file and workspace filtering, and output format.
Category flags include symbols, specs, and documents, defaulting to all if none are provided.
Filters are applied at the store level before the limit.
Search ranking must prioritize exact matches on primary identities regardless of generic relevance scores.
The command delegates to CodeGraphProvider search methods for symbols, specs, and documents.
Results are ordered by descending relevance score.
Search results must include preview context with the matched text and a 1-based line range.
Symbol previews are derived from persisted source-file content.
Spec previews are derived from matched spec content or description fallback.
Document previews are derived from matched document content.
The command validates the kind option against allowed SymbolKind values before query execution.
The command fails fast if the shared graph indexing lock is active.
Text mode groups results by category with a header showing the limit.
Each result in text mode renders an identity block and a separate snippet block with a line range indicator.
Snippets are wrapped in custom markers to avoid markdown formatting collisions.
Text-mode symbol snippets use line-oriented source context with normalized indentation.
Spec and document snippets are match-centered and rendered in a separate snippet block.
The command exits with code 3 if the provider cannot be opened.
The command accepts filters for kind, file, workspace, exclude-path, and exclude-workspace.

constraints: The CLI does not contain search logic and delegates to CodeGraphProvider methods.
Search depends on the active graph-store backend having prepared its search indexes.
The command checks the shared graph indexing lock before opening the provider.
The process.exit(0) pattern is required after closing the provider.

scenarios.name: Search both categories by default
when: specd graph search is run without category flags
then: both symbol and spec results are returned

scenarios.name: Results ranked by relevance
given: specs matching the query to varying degrees
then: higher-ranked results have stronger relevance scores
